response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a capable full-stack engineer with genuine breadth across web, mobile, infrastructure, and embedded systems, and his entrepreneurial track record with GISCollective shows independent initiative. However, the role demands a Director-level individual contributor with recognized, deep AI expertise and a demonstrated history of shipping AI-powered platforms at scale — criteria this resume does not meet. His AI exposure is limited to a two-month-old Staff Engineer role involving MCP tooling, far short of the strategic depth, customer advisory capacity, and AI thought-leadership the position requires. The absence of an advanced degree, cloud platform experience, and any evidence of influencing org-level AI strategy further widen the gap.
strengths: Genuine polyglot engineer with 15+ years spanning embedded systems, high-throughput microservices, GIS platforms, and full-stack product work — unusually broad technical foundation.
Founded and still runs GISCollective, an open-source geo-data platform used in production by NGOs, demonstrating entrepreneurial execution and product ownership.
Active community contributor through public talks (FOSS4G), workshops, and open-source libraries, showing some ability to share knowledge and build external credibility.
concerns: AI expertise is nascent and narrow: the only AI-related work is two months at re:cinq building MCP tooling — there is no history of AI product strategy, AI platform architecture, or advising customers on AI adoption.
Seniority and scope mismatch: career history is individual contributor at Staff/Senior level with no evidence of Director-level scope, cross-org influence, or executive stakeholder engagement.

Education gap: holds two Bachelor's degrees (CS and Fine Arts/Photography) with no advanced degree in an AI-related field, which is a preferred qualifier for this role and signals limited formal AI research grounding.
